briefdescription.para: A libxml automata description.

detaileddescription.para: It can be compiled into a regexp

briefdescription.para: A state in the automata description.
Create a new automata.

simplesect.para: the new object or NULL in case of failure

briefdescription.para: Free an automata.

parameterdescription.para: an automata

briefdescription.para: Initial state lookup.

parameterdescription.para: an automata

simplesect.para: the initial state of the automata

briefdescription.para: Makes that state a final state.

parameterdescription.para: an automata
a state in this automata

simplesect.para: 0 or -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Create a new disconnected state in the automata.

parameterdescription.para: an automata

simplesect.para: the new state or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Add a transition.

detaileddescription.para: If to is NULL, this creates first a new target state in the automata and then adds a transition from the from state to the target state activated by the value of token

parameterdescription.para: an automata
the starting point of the transition
the target point of the transition or NULL
the input string associated to that transition
data passed to the callback function if the transition is activated

simplesect.para: the target state or NULL in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlAutomata *am, xmlAutomataState *from, xmlAutomataState *to, const xmlChar *token, const xmlChar *token2, void *data)
memberdef.name: xmlAutomataNewTransition2

briefdescription.para: If to is NULL, this creates first a new target state in the automata and then adds a transition from the from state to the target state activated by the value of token

parameterdescription.para: an automata
the starting point of the transition
the target point of the transition or NULL
the first input string associated to that transition
the second input string associated to that transition
data passed to the callback function if the transition is activated

simplesect.para: the target state or NULL in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlAutomata *am, xmlAutomataState *from, xmlAutomataState *to, const xmlChar *token, const xmlChar *token2, void *data)
memberdef.name: xmlAutomataNewNegTrans

briefdescription.para: If to is NULL, this creates first a new target state in the automata and then adds a transition from the from state to the target state activated by any value except (token,token2) Note that if token2 is not NULL, then (X, NULL) won't match to follow the semantic of XSD ##other.

parameterdescription.para: an automata
the starting point of the transition
the target point of the transition or NULL
the first input string associated to that transition
the second input string associated to that transition
data passed to the callback function if the transition is activated

simplesect.para: the target state or NULL in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlAutomata *am, xmlAutomataState *from, xmlAutomataState *to, const xmlChar *token, int min, int max, void *data)
memberdef.name: xmlAutomataNewCountTrans

briefdescription.para: If to is NULL, this creates first a new target state in the automata and then adds a transition from the from state to the target state activated by a succession of input of value token and whose number is between min and max

parameterdescription.para: an automata
the starting point of the transition
the target point of the transition or NULL
the input string associated to that transition
the minimum successive occurrences of token
the maximum successive occurrences of token
data associated to the transition

simplesect.para: the target state or NULL in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlAutomata *am, xmlAutomataState *from, xmlAutomataState *to, const xmlChar *token, const xmlChar *token2, int min, int max, void *data)
memberdef.name: xmlAutomataNewCountTrans2

briefdescription.para: If to is NULL, this creates first a new target state in the automata and then adds a transition from the from state to the target state activated by a succession of input of value token and token2 and whose number is between min and max

parameterdescription.para: an automata
the starting point of the transition
the target point of the transition or NULL
the input string associated to that transition
the second input string associated to that transition
the minimum successive occurrences of token
the maximum successive occurrences of token
data associated to the transition

simplesect.para: the target state or NULL in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlAutomata *am, xmlAutomataState *from, xmlAutomataState *to, const xmlChar *token, int min, int max, void *data)
memberdef.name: xmlAutomataNewOnceTrans

briefdescription.para: If to is NULL, this creates first a new target state in the automata and then adds a transition from the from state to the target state activated by a succession of input of value token and whose number is between min and max, moreover that transition can only be crossed once.

parameterdescription.para: an automata
the starting point of the transition
the target point of the transition or NULL
the input string associated to that transition
the minimum successive occurrences of token
the maximum successive occurrences of token
data associated to the transition

simplesect.para: the target state or NULL in case of error

memberdef.argsstring: (xmlAutomata *am, xmlAutomataState *from, xmlAutomataState *to, const xmlChar *token, const xmlChar *token2, int min, int max, void *data)
memberdef.name: xmlAutomataNewOnceTrans2

briefdescription.para: If to is NULL, this creates first a new target state in the automata and then adds a transition from the from state to the target state activated by a succession of input of value token and token2 and whose number is between min and max, moreover that transition can only be crossed once.

parameterdescription.para: an automata
the starting point of the transition
the target point of the transition or NULL
the input string associated to that transition
the second input string associated to that transition
the minimum successive occurrences of token
the maximum successive occurrences of token
data associated to the transition

simplesect.para: the target state or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: If to is NULL, this creates first a new target state in the automata and then adds a an ALL transition from the from state to the target state.

detaileddescription.para: That transition is an epsilon transition allowed only when all transitions from the from node have been activated.

parameterdescription.para: an automata
the starting point of the transition
the target point of the transition or NULL
allow to transition if not all all transitions have been activated

simplesect.para: the target state or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: If to is NULL, this creates first a new target state in the automata and then adds an epsilon transition from the from state to the target state.

parameterdescription.para: an automata
the starting point of the transition
the target point of the transition or NULL

simplesect.para: the target state or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: If to is NULL, this creates first a new target state in the automata and then adds an epsilon transition from the from state to the target state which will increment the counter provided.

parameterdescription.para: an automata
the starting point of the transition
the target point of the transition or NULL
the counter associated to that transition

simplesect.para: the target state or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: If to is NULL, this creates first a new target state in the automata and then adds an epsilon transition from the from state to the target state which will be allowed only if the counter is within the right range.

parameterdescription.para: an automata
the starting point of the transition
the target point of the transition or NULL
the counter associated to that transition

simplesect.para: the target state or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Create a new counter.

parameterdescription.para: an automata
the minimal value on the counter
the maximal value on the counter

simplesect.para: the counter number or -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: Compile the automata into a Reg Exp ready for being executed.

detaileddescription.para: The automata should be free after this point.

parameterdescription.para: an automata

simplesect.para: the compiled regexp or NULL in case of error

briefdescription.para: Checks if an automata is determinist.

parameterdescription.para: an automata

simplesect.para: 1 if true, 0 if not, and -1 in case of error

briefdescription.para: API to build regexp automata.

detaileddescription.para: These are internal functions and shouldn't be used.

simplesect.para: See Copyright for the status of this software.
Daniel Veillard
